expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states the case focus and covers it directly. In result.md: "Case focus (must be explicit): Canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios" and it details the Phase 4b requirement to "Group ... into the canonical top-layer taxonomy" and "Preserve scenario granularity (anti-compression)". execution_notes.md also says: "Explicitly assessed the Phase 4b requirement: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: The produced output is framed as a Phase 4b contract assessment and is organized around Phase 4b requirements and taxonomy. result.md begins with "P4B-LAYERING-001 — Phase 4b Contract Benchmark Assessment" and "Primary phase/checkpoint: Phase 4b" and then enumerates "What Phase 4b is required to do" including canonical top-layer categories and Phase 4b-specific constraints. This aligns the output to Phase 4b (even though it notes Phase 4b run artifacts are missing).
